title: schizocarphus hispidulus
intro: Schizocarphus hispidulus, also known as the hairy burreed or burreed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in wet environments and areas with moist soil. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Schizocarphus hispidulus include relieving coughs and colds, as well as providing relief from sinus congestion and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
